text: Voter Turnout: 18th Lok Sabha 2024. National: 65.79%. Highest: Lakshadweep 84.5%, Tripura 81.8%. Lowest: Bihar 58.2%, UP 56.9%. Total electors: 96.88 crore. EVM-VVPAT in all booths. Phased voting over 7 phases.
Voter Turnout: 17th Lok Sabha 2019. National: 67.4% (highest ever in Lok Sabha). Highest: Lakshadweep 84.6%. Lowest: Bihar 57.3%. Total electors: 91.19 crore. 7 phases. 10.39 lakh polling stations.
Voter Turnout: 16th Lok Sabha 2014. National: 66.4%. Highest: Nagaland 87.8%. Lowest: J&K 49.5%. Total electors: 83.41 crore. 9 phases. Record 55.38 crore votes cast. NOTA option introduced.

Voter Turnout: Five State Elections 2023 (MP, Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Mizoram). Average: 73.8%. State elections typically have higher turnout than Lok Sabha. Mizoram: 81%. MP: 75.6%. Rajasthan: 74.6%.

Voter Turnout Historical Trend: 1st LS (1951): 45.7%. 5th LS (1971): 55.3%. 10th LS (1991): 56.9%. 15th LS (2009): 58.2%. 17th LS (2019): 67.4% (peak). Trend: Steady increase since 2009, driven by awareness campaigns, EPIC cards, improved booth access.
